title: Song To Diana by Ben Jonson — Nature Poetry Lines
content:
Queen and huntress, chaste and fair,
Now the sun is laid to sleep,
Seated in thy silver chair
State in wonted manner keep:
Hesperus entreats thy light,
Goddess excellently bright.

Earth, let not thy envious shade
meaning: "Song To Diana" by Ben Jonson is a nature poem consisting of 20 lines. The poet explores themes of nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Queen and huntress, chaste and fair, Now the sun is laid to sleep,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Ben Jonson's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
